segments.text: Now the word of the LORD came to me, saying,

Go and proclaim in the hearing of Jerusalem that this is what the LORD says:
‘I remember the devotion of your youth, your love as a bride, how you followed Me in the wilderness, in a land not sown.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Israel was holy to the LORD, the firstfruits of His harvest. All who devoured herfound themselves guilty; disaster came upon them,’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD.
Hear the word of the LORD, O house of Jacob, and all you families of the house of Israel.
This is what the LORD says:
What fault did your fathers find in Methat they strayed so far from Me, and followed worthless idols, and became worthless themselves?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: They did not ask, ‘Where is the LORD who brought us up from the land of Egypt, who led us through the wilderness, through a land of deserts and pits, a land of drought and darkness, a land where no one travels and no one lives?’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I brought you into a fertile landto eat its fruit and bounty, but you came and defiled My land, and made My inheritance detestable.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The priests did not ask,‘Where is the LORD?’The experts in the law no longer knew Me, and the leaders rebelled against Me. The prophets prophesied by Baal and followed useless idols.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Therefore, I will contend with you again, declares the LORD, and I will bring a caseagainst your children’s children.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Cross over to the coasts of Cyprus and take a look; send to Kedar and consider carefully; see if there has ever been anything like this:
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Has a nation ever changed its gods, though they are no gods at all? Yet My people have exchanged their Glory for useless idols.
segments.speaker: the Lord

footnotes.text: + Hebrew of Shihor, a branch of the Nile River

segments.text: Be stunned by this, O heavens; be shocked and utterly appalled,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD.

For My people have committed two evils:
They have forsaken Me, the fountain of living water, and they have dug their own cisterns—broken cisterns that cannot hold water.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Is Israel a slave? Was he born into slavery?
Why then has he become prey?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: The young lions have roared at him; they have growled with a loud voice. They have laid waste his land; his cities lie in ruins, without inhabitant.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: The men of Memphis and Tahpanheshave shaved the crown of your head.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
Have you not brought this on yourselfby forsaking the LORD your God
when He led you in the way?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Now what will you gain on your way to Egyptto drink the waters of the Nile? What will you gain on your way to Assyriato drink the waters of the Euphrates?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Your own evil will discipline you; your own apostasies will reprimand you. Consider and realizehow evil and bitter it isfor you to forsake the LORD your God and to have no fear of Me,
segments.speaker: The LORD GOD

segments.text: declares the Lord GOD of Hosts.

For long ago you broke your yoke and tore off your chains,
saying, ‘I will not serve!’Indeed, on every high hill and under every green tree
you lay down as a prostitute.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I had planted you like a choice vinefrom the very best seed. How could you turn yourself before Meinto a rotten, wild vine?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Although you wash with lye and use an abundance of soap, the stain of your guiltis still before Me,
segments.speaker: The LORD GOD

segments.text: declares the Lord GOD.
How can you say, ‘I am not defiled; I have not run after the Baals’? Look at your behavior in the valley; acknowledge what you have done. You are a swift young she-camelgalloping here and there,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
a wild donkey at home in the wilderness, sniffing the wind in the heat of her desire.
Who can restrain her passion? All who seek her need not weary themselves; in mating season they will find her.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
You should have kept your feet from going bare and your throat from being thirsty. But you said, ‘It is hopeless! For I love foreign gods,
and I must go after them.’
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: As the thief is ashamed when he is caught, so the house of Israel is disgraced. They, their kings, their officials, their priests, and their prophets
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: say to a tree, ‘You are my father,’and to a stone, ‘You gave me birth.’For they have turned their backs to Me and not their faces, yet in the time of trouble they beg,‘Rise up and save us!’
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
But where are the gods you made for yourselves? Let them rise up in your time of trouble
and save you if they can; for your gods are as numerousas your cities, O Judah.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Why do you bring a case against Me? You have all rebelled against Me,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD.
I have struck your sons in vain; they accepted no discipline. Your own sword has devoured your prophetslike a voracious lion.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: You people of this generation, consider the word of the LORD:

Have I been a wilderness to Israelor a land of dense darkness? Why do My people say,‘We are free to roam;
we will come to You no more’?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Does a maiden forget her jewelryor a bride her wedding sash? Yet My people have forgotten Mefor days without number.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
How skillfully you pursue love! Even the most immoral of women
could learn from your ways.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
Moreover, your skirts are stainedwith the blood of the innocent poor,
though you did not find them breaking in.
But in spite of all these things
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
you say, ‘I am innocent.
Surely His anger will turn from me.’Behold, I will judge you, because you say, ‘I have not sinned.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: How unstable you are, constantly changing your ways! You will be disappointed by Egyptjust as you were by Assyria.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Moreover, you will leave that placewith your hands on your head, for the LORD has rejected those you trust; you will not prosper by their help.
segments.speaker: the Lord
